team.name: VisuLive
team.description: Flagship visual-reactive show development, proof operations, runtime extraction, and evidence-led capability growth.

projects.name: Proof Backbone & Clean Workspace
projects.summary: Make the active rewrite branch clean, proof-gated, and ready for fresh serious capture before push, deploy, staging, or capability expansion.
projects.description: This project is the operating backbone for the next VisuLive wave: clean committed workspace, strict proof and release gates, trustworthy evidence flow, Linear-backed execution, then fresh serious proof. Current branch decisions must come from fresh valid run packages, not stale April evidence.

milestones.name: Clean Source Boundary
milestones.description: Generated evidence and heavy release artifacts are ignored or externalized while lightweight provenance stays trackable.

milestones.name: Review Finding Closure
milestones.description: All proof, release, runtime-truth, and analyzer review findings are closed with regression checks.

milestones.name: Fresh Serious Proof
milestones.description: Primary benchmark and operator-trust runs are collected through the hard-gated Proof Wave path.

milestones.name: Evidence-Led Next Development
milestones.description: Fresh recommendations decide governance correction versus PostSystem readiness and later capability growth.

labels.name: proof-gate
labels.description: Blocks or validates serious proof, proof-pack, benchmark, or release readiness.

labels.name: evidence
labels.description: Capture, run-package, catalog, proof report, analyzer, or recommendation work.

labels.name: release
labels.description: Manual release, deployment verification, preservation, or production-hosting work.

labels.name: runtime
labels.description: Flagship runtime ownership, system extraction, orchestration, or scene-shell reduction.

labels.name: operator-ux
labels.description: Backstage, diagnostics, proof arming, controls, and human proof workflow.

labels.name: docs
labels.description: Agent canon, runbooks, project status, and documentation truth.

labels.name: artifact-policy
labels.description: Git hygiene, generated artifacts, evidence storage, preservation boundaries.

labels.name: governance
labels.description: Authority, lighting, particles, composition safety, and proof-led correction.

labels.name: linear-ops
labels.description: Linear workspace, issue templates, status workflow, and agent operating system.

states.name: Backlog
states.meaning: Valid work item, not selected for current local execution.

states.name: Ready
states.meaning: Has acceptance criteria, owner lane, proof target, and no unresolved scope ambiguity.

states.name: In Progress
states.meaning: Currently being changed locally or by an assigned agent.

states.name: Blocked
states.meaning: Waiting on operator capture, connector auth, external account access, or an explicit dependency.

states.name: Review
states.meaning: Code/docs complete, verification pending or review findings being checked.

states.name: Done
states.meaning: Merged or committed with verification and checkpoint.

agentRules: Every coding issue must include owner lane, acceptance checks, proof target, and no-go boundaries.
Every proof issue must name the scenario, source mode, no-touch requirement, and promotion rule.
Do not create capability-growth issues that bypass fresh current proof.
Use Linear as planning and traceability, not as evidence truth; evidence truth remains run packages and proof reports.

issues.title: Clean artifact boundaries and ignored generated evidence
issues.description:
Generated evidence and heavy release outputs must not dirty source Git while lightweight provenance stays trackable.

Owner lane: Evidence / Capture / Analyzer.

Acceptance:
- Generated SQLite catalogs and run payloads do not dirty Git.
- Heavy v1 preserved artifacts stay out of source Git.
- Lightweight release records, checksums, manifests, and docs remain trackable.
- `git status --short` is clean after proof/report commands.

issues.title: Commit structured proof-ready workspace stack
issues.description:
Convert accumulated local work into a clean, reviewable commit stack before fresh serious proof.

Owner lane: Repo Operations.

Acceptance:
- Dirty workspace is converted into focused commits.
- `git status --short` is empty.
- `npm run proof:preflight` passes without `--allow-dirty`.
- Checkpoint records commit stack and next proof steps.

issues.title: Close release/proof gate findings
issues.description:
Release verification must not pass when proof gates fail.

Owner lane: Release / Evidence.

Acceptance:
- `release:verify` requires current benchmark truth and strict proof-pack gates.
- `npm run benchmark:validate -- --require-current` fails until active/current-canonical benchmark exists.
- `npm run proof-pack -- --limit 1 --strict` fails until a current-proof-eligible run exists.
- `npm run release:verify` fails until strict proof and benchmark gates pass.

issues.title: Close run journal/build identity findings
issues.description:
Serious proof must reject weak, dirty, or unknown build identity and must not serialize false screenshot references.

Owner lane: Evidence / Operator UX.

Acceptance:
- Serious proof rejects dev, unverified, dirty, unknown, or invalid build identity.
- Run metadata uses the shared build identity validator.
- Checkpoint still references are registered only after successful save.
- Save failure invalidates the proof run with explicit reasons.

issues.title: Close run package/catalog/analyzer findings
issues.description:
Run packages and evidence query tooling must be safe and chronologically honest.

Owner lane: Evidence / Capture / Analyzer.

Acceptance:
- Run package promotion refuses overwrites.
- Recommendation gates can pass, warn, or fail.
- `after-commit` evidence queries use commit/build timestamps, not lexicographic hash comparison.
- Catalog can answer recent failure pattern queries without misleading ordering.

issues.title: Close runtime/docs anthology truth findings
issues.description:
Machine-readable and prose canon must match current runtime ownership.

Owner lane: Runtime / Docs.

Acceptance:
- Machine-readable anthology catalog reflects HeroSystem owned lifecycle status.
- AGENTS and deployment docs match current runtime and release-gate truth.
- Future agents are directed toward PostSystem and proof-led governance, not stale facade assumptions.

issues.title: Collect clean primary-benchmark proof
issues.description:
Collect the first serious current proof run on the hardened backbone.

Owner lane: Operator / Evidence.

Acceptance:
- Run uses `npm run dev:proof`.
- Scenario is `Primary benchmark`.
- Source is PC Audio.
- Run is no-touch, current-proof-eligible, screenshot-backed, and reviewed.
- `npm run proof:current`, `npm run evidence:index`, and `npm run run:review -- --run-id <runId>` complete.

issues.title: Collect clean operator-trust proof
issues.description:
Collect proof that a normal operator can arm and run the show without hidden manual intervention.

Owner lane: Operator UX / Evidence.

Acceptance:
- Scenario is `Operator trust`.
- Operator-trust scenario clears no-touch and intervention requirements.
- Run package is reviewed and promoted or archived as a whole package.
- Any invalidation reason produces actionable operator recovery guidance.

issues.title: Review proof recommendations and decide governance follow-up
issues.description:
Use fresh recommendation JSON and review notes to choose the next narrow correction.

Owner lane: Governance / Evidence.

Acceptance:
- Recommendation JSON is reviewed.
- Overbright, ring persistence, chamber presence, and world takeover outcomes determine the next correction.
- If proof is weak, next pass is AuthorityGovernor, LightingSystem, and ParticleSystem only.
- If proof is strong, move to PostSystem readiness.

issues.title: Decide PostSystem readiness after authority proof
issues.description:
Decide whether the runtime is ready for the next structural extraction.

Owner lane: Runtime / Governance.

Acceptance:
- If authority proof holds, next structural target is `PostSystem`.
- If authority proof fails, perform one focused governance correction first.
- No capability-growth issue starts until this decision is recorded.
